title: Papa Mole's Lunar Expedition
summary: Papa Mole, Disco, and Thisco blast off in a snack-filled rocket ship for a grand expedition to the moon. Exploring the gray, cratered landscape in specialized oxygen-equipped spacesuits, the trio experiences the thrill of low gravity, bouncing high into the air. Their adventure takes a surprising turn when they discover mysterious moon worms living in a giant crater. After a quick retreat to the ship and a narrow escape from the wiggling creatures, the tired explorers return safely to Earth just in time for bed.
audioFilename: Stars & Stories: Moles on the Moon.m4a

highlightQuote.text: Just keep telling the story and you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who's there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Papa Mole, said Disco and Thisco. What are you up to today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Explore.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what do you want to explore this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Under.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Under what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just keep telling the story and you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I thought we were asking Papa Mole what he wanted to explore.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But keep telling the story and then we'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And Papa Mole says, "Today we're going to the moon." And Disco and Thisco said, "The moon for real?" And Papa Mole said, "Yep. We've got a little rocket ship and we've got a bag of snacks and we're going to fly right up there and explore the moon. What do you think it's going to be like?" And what did they say? What do you think we'll find up there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Need oxygen.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're right, said Papa Mole. We're going to have spacesuits with oxygen. We do need that. What else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And water bottles in case our oxygen runs out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And water bottles too, yes. That's a good point. And what else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A crow's nest and a telescope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, our rocket ship doesn't have those, but we'll we can add them real quick. Okay, I added them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually we don't need a crow's nest.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Usually that's just on a boat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or a telescope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well the telescope is good to have so you can see things that are far away from the rocket ship.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, so maybe you could see Mars with a telescope.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's a great idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually how about we could go to Mars, Papa Mole?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well tonight we're going to the moon. Maybe tomorrow we could go to Mars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Everybody all aboard the rocket ship. We'll do our countdown. Five, four, three, two, one, blast off! And the rocket ship shot straight up away from the forest through the clouds, up out of the atmosphere of Planet Earth and all the way to the beautiful shining white moon. And Papa Mole said, "We're almost there. Look out the window, Disco. Look out the window, Thisco. Do you see the moon out there? What does it look like?" And what did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, "Wow, it's so bright. It's all gray and white, bright in the sunlight with deep dark shadows. It looks like there's no air. It's just gray and white dirt shining in the sun and there's big mountains. But what are those big holes?" And Thisco said, "Oh, oh, oh, I know. They're big round holes because..." Do you know what they are?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They're called craters. And Papa Mole said, "That's right. Do you know what makes a crater?" And Thisco said, "No, I don't know." Do you know, Iz?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A crater is the big hole from when a rock flying through space hits the moon. Boom! It hits it so hard that it makes a big round hole in the moon and that's called a crater. And the moon is covered with craters. Next time we go and look at it up in the sky we can we can see the craters. It has really big craters and small craters and they're all over it because the moon has been around for a long time and lots of little rocks have hit it and made craters. And Disco said, "Wow, can we land in one?" And Papa Mole said, "Okay, we'll land the rocket ship in that little crater. It looks like just the right size for us." Boop! And they landed their ship. He said, "Okay, now we can go out and explore but remember everyone, gotta wear your spacesuits and put on your oxygen and make sure you have your water bottle too." And he went click, click, click and put on his suit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And a problem.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Disco said, "I have I have a problem."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, I have an itch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just itch it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well I can't because I'm in my spacesuit, so I can't reach it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Then you can take it off in the rocket.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh okay, good idea. And zip, zip, zip, click, click, click, pop, pop, pop. Disco jumped out of his spacesuit and scratched his knee. Ah, whew! Okay, now I'll go back in. Pop, pop, pop, zip, zip, zip, click, click, click. Now I'm ready to go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's go. And Disco and Thisco and Papa Mole climbed out the door of their rocket ship and down the little steps until they were walking on the moon. And as they walked, they were bouncing in the air. Boing, boing, boing. And Disco said, "Why are we bouncing like that?" And Papa Mole said, "Well, the moon is a special place. It's a smaller planet than the Earth and so it has less gravity, which pulls us... the gravity is what pulls us down to the earth and makes us fall. We don't fall as fast on the moon. And when you jump, you can jump up higher. And when you walk, you bounce. It's like you weigh less on the moon." And Disco said, "Woohoo!" and he went boing and he jumped high up in the air, higher than he could down on Earth. And Papa Mole said, "Is there anything else you want to explore?" And what did they say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's explore that big crater down there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that other crater. Okay. And Papa Mole said, "Let's go over the lip of this crater," because a crater's a kind of a hole, but you can imagine it being like a bowl shape, and you have to climb up over the edge. And then they went down into the next crater over, and it was much bigger. And what did they see there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some moon worms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some moon worms and the worm wiggled away and then we don't want to get eaten.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole said, "Wow, moon worms! I didn't know there were worms on the moon." And there aren't really, but in our story there are. And Papa Mole said, "Okay, I don't want to run out of oxygen, so we better go back to the rocket ship so we can get safely home." And so they climbed back into the little crater where they started, they went back to their rocket ship, and they opened the door and they blasted off. Pshhh! And they went all the way back to the Earth, and then they landed right near their house and they got out, and they were very tired, so they went to bed. And that's the end of our story.
